I am heartbroken by this game. I waited so long for another modern city builder game, and was hopeful for this game to be Cities: Skylines's successor, but even after more than 2 years, the game still has too many issues that still make the previous game preferable in my opinion.  The good things:  - Road building is amazing. They did away with the restrictions and issues the previous game had. You now have more road options, parking, more interchanges, roundabouts. It feels way better than CS1. You have much more freedom when placing roads, and road anarchy is no longer a must have for this game.  - Modding has been improved. You no longer need to own the game on steam, but you need a Paradox Account to download mods. Developers have added free content packs that contain themed buildings and zoning. The game also has better integrations if you want to create and publish mods. There are still way less mods than the previous game had, but it can only improve with time.  - Zoning is better, you can zone with specific building theme, you have a lot more zoning options, like low rent, or shore front properties, Chinese high density, NA medium density etc.  - Service building now serve a larger area but you have to keep track of their service capacity  - You can do a lot more micro details now, like picking building models/colors  The bad things:  - The game is still lacking a lot of content that the previous game had. Knowing how paradox operates, we'll probably get it through DLCs over time. Which is a shame because CS1 had that content for free (no campuses, no custom airports, no specialized industry buildings, less unique buildings, no trroleybuses, no monorail etc.)  - Simulation is still glitchy. It has improved drastically compared to CS1. Citizens are now using public transports, new road options really help with traffic. However there are still glitches: car will disapear, vehicles are still choosing the wrong lane until the very last moment, and more.  - The map is WAY SMALLER than CS1. I believe this was done to limit the simulation problems that appear with larger cities. Simcity 2013 tried to do the same, and we know how it ended.  - There are outstanding graphic issues, like roads not visually connecting, or glitched road decals, but they're less annoying  Regarding the performance issues, the base game in Medium runs at 40-45fps while sitting on an empty map. And that's with DLSS on. You can always put the graphics settings lower, which will make the game look worse than CS1, while getting worse performances. As you start building the graphics performance don't really degrade, but it's still annoying that the game is lagging so much. There are also terrible ghosting issues with vehicles, but that can be dealt with by turning off DLSS. The simulation is WAY heavier on the CPU compared to CS1, but it's multithreaded. It feels smoother below 100k pop, but after that it's basically impossible to play at max simulation speed. But that's mostly a tradeoff, because now the simulation is more detailed. At around 500k pop, all CPU cores are maxed out and it becomes pretty much impossible to play while simulation is running.  Anyway I attached my specs which should be well beyond the 'Recommended' PC specs.  That said, the game is still receiving updates, and I wish for this game to improve, otherwise we'll be stuck with playing Simcity 4 and CS1 and these games are getting old.

Typically, my reviews vary between professional and unprofessional. This is usually just for games I play for fun and ones I’m genuinely reviewing.  I don’t mean to be rude, but this game simply cannot be reviewed as a fun experience.  The game completely breaks if you have DX11, which is very common. The game seems good at first glance, but it’s not. DX12 and above won’t experience this, but if you have DX11 or lower, your game turns into a strobe light when doing anything. It’s very laggy and poorly optimized for a game this big. To be fair, the game does have some good features and a solid core idea. You can see what it’s trying to be, and on paper it sounds great. But the execution just isn’t there yet, and all those good ideas are buried under technical issues and poor performance.  Most of the DLCs are not good and are basically mods that you have to pay $5–8 for.  I’ve played other city builders before. Mainly, I’ve played Manor Lords (by Hooded Horse), and this game is very good if you’re into that. But be warned: your PC should be able to run this. That’s very important.  **NOTE:** The new ownership is trying to make the game better for players, but they have recently tanked the graphics.  While this review does not recommend the game overall, I would still recommend it if you’re a dedicated fan and have a PC that can run it well. However, I strongly suggest using performance mods and visual improvement mods to get a decent experience.

If you are considering buying this game you need to forget about any screenshots or videos you've seen.   The new developer (Iceflake) absolutely nuked the graphical quality; severely oversaturating the colours, and downgrading the lighting and shadow quality. In fact, I would go so far as to say that the screenshots and videos on the store page are incredibly misleading (false advertising) and no longer reflect whatsoever the product you are buying. I mean, it actually looks worse than Cities Skylines 1 now.   I did enjoy the ~100 hours I got out of this game, but unless Iceflake hires someone with eyeballs to revert the graphical and aesthetic quality back to the original, I can't recommend this game and can't even play it without feeling ashamed of myself.  I'm all for making improvements but when a patch is actually a downgrade to something unrecognizable, and you don't have the option to revert to a previous game version, it feels like your money is being stolen.  Edit: I must, in good conscience update my review after the latest Hotfix - Patch 1.5.5f1. Kudos to Iceflake for admitting they made a mistake, hearing the complaints and reverting the graphical aesthetics back to its original state, in a timely manner.   Is this game worth buying? For me, I must say I have enjoyed my time so far, despite its many flaws. The game is by no means perfect, but if you are patient and enjoy using your creativity, there is quite a bit to explore here. However, I must leave my review in the 'not recommended' category for one main reason: the price.   This game is ridiculously expensive. It does not go on sale very often and when it does it is an insignificant ~15% discount. Also, selling a DLC at 40% of the cost of the base game is absolutely absurd. In short, this game and its DLC's are far too expensive and should be selling at 50% of it's current price.

I don't really understand this sequel.  I mean... it's... bigger?  Sort of.  The maps are bigger.  But... then what?  Why is this a thing?  It seems like a mechanical downgrade in many ways, doing nothing to enrich the underlying civil or logistical simulation.  And it fails to address my number one gripe with all city builders to date... no blending from urban to uninhabited.  There's no rural boundary.  Yes, you can mimic it by very tediously trying to carefully recreate the basic aesthetic of the city giving way to countryside gradually, but the game doesn't help, and the mechanics don't work well for it.  The result is the same as ever... little islands of city that look like little dioramas sitting awkwardly in a barren landscape with no meaningful purpose or interaction within a larger political, economic, and logistical landscape.  This game is the manifestation of stagnation.  Oh yeah, and it runs like crap.

For those that have played the previous game and explored all the mods, dlcs, and custom content, you will find this game lacking in all aspects. Now it is in a state where it barely matches Cities Skylines 1 during launch. Mods are supported and have qol improvements, custom assets were finally added after an year, and new DLCs are dropping. However, the game still has many bugs, most of the gamebreaking ones have been solved, and the optimization is still poor. On the positive, the buildings look nice, especially the content packs and custom assets, the road building is light years ahead of vanilla Cities Skylines 1, and the new developers are actively working to fix the game and improve it. I would wait before getting this game, or wait for a good sale. At its current price, I cannot recommend it just yet.

Not worth the price unless the base game is fixed or you're desperate to build roads

I really tried to like Cities: Skylines 2, but unfortunately I just can’t. It’s bug after bug, crash after crash.  I don’t see much hope even with changes in developers. Since the launch of Cities: Skylines 1, they kept saying they would fix the traffic system, and they never really did — they just kept pushing the problem forward.  Regarding Cities: Skylines 2, I think many famous YouTubers who had early access and did gameplay share part of the responsibility. At the time, they said they were helping the developers with ideas and suggestions. So why were the optimization issues and so many bugs not properly reported? Why didn’t anyone suggest delaying the release instead of launching the game in what feels like a beta state?  In the end, I don’t see a future for this game. I don’t believe it will ever have a comeback like No Man’s Sky or Cyberpunk 2077.

[h1]A Traffic Jam Simulator that gives me PTSD.[/h1] [i]I play on an old PC with the lowest settings, and while the crashes have finally stopped, I still save every hour just in case. It’s a road-management puzzle where bicycles are now your biggest nightmare.[/i]  [h1]Roads, Traffic, and Panic Saves[/h1]  [b]The Performance Struggle[/b] I’m running this on an older computer, so I have to keep all the settings on "Low." It used to crash quite a bit, but it has been stable lately. Even so, the game has given me "Save-PTSD"—I still manually save every hour because I don’t trust it yet!  [b]The Gameplay[/b] This isn't just a city builder; it’s a "Traffic Jam Simulator." You spend your entire time managing road maps and obsessing over how people move through every single block. The new bicycle add-on is the final touch: it takes something that is already a "pain in the neck" in real life and turns it into a core game feature.  [b]Verdict[/b] If you love solving logistical nightmares and drawing roads, you'll like this. Just make sure you have a good "Save" finger.  [b]Score: 6/10 — My city is beautiful, but my traffic is a disaster.[/b]

Big fan of skylines, I held out on my review HOPING things would change. If its on sale for like $10-$20 grab it, otherwise stick to the original.  DO NOT BUY UNTIL YOU READ:::::::::::: BUY OG   what i loved: (compared to cities: Skylines) - Option for dual road building - Great graphics - Better options for zoning, multiple zoning options - Utilities are now under roads, which is wonderful hey: -DLC content seems lacking and extremely overpriced.  why I'd stick with Cities: Skylines (the OG) - Far less hard drive space for a better gaming experience -Crashes are FAR less -Usable and user friendly map editor (CS2's map editor is extremely over complicated for casual editors) -Assets are sized appropriately  -Bridges. you can have them AND have shipping under them WITHOUT a DLC.  -Navigating everything is vastly easier, and common key changed in CS2.  -DLC packs early on actually tried and were good because they cared about gaming experience.  -Great mods that wont break your game -ACTUALLY worth your money.   Map editor: - CS1 has a map editor that needs some learning curve, BUT CS2 map editor needs an ENTIRE user manual. You will be using google to know how to do basic map editing things. Finding a specific road, airplane path and even making your bridges high enough are a struggle in this game. It went from a visual to a non-visual in menu style. Not so great for most of us who love the original map editor.    In summation; DO NOT BUY THIS GAME. They took a good game and made/released an unfinished SECOND game. We all know how that goes.  Buy the OG. it offers more, wont crash and you can actually edit a playable map in under 2 hours.

A couple of years in with Cities Skylines 2 and why I struggle to play it now...  I've invested 100s of hours into this game so why a negative review. In a word, performance.  Staggeringly, despite a massive PC upgrade and generational leaps in my graphics cards since I bought this game the week of its release a few years back. I now pack a RTX5080, 32gb of Ram and an AMD X3D CPU, a gaming powerhouse that runs DCS in VR at 70fps on high settings, and yet  I still cannot get Cities Skylines 2 to load and run acceptably without it being in potato mode.  When the game came out I played it a lot, in those days running at RX580. Obviously then I was running it at minimum settings. However, it was playable despite the crashes and need to restart or run without mods. Shortly after I upgraded to RTX3080, more RAM and a faster CPU and despite having DLSS at my disposal, 12GB of VRAM it still ran like a dog on anything but the lowest settings.  Now I am running a beast of a PC and I've tried tweaks and settings and everything under the sun, but it is truly still as unplayable today as it was on day one, if it loads at all. This week I tried to start a new city, got it loaded after 3 x attempts. Started doing the roads and underground bits- running like a dog so made all the graphics at the lowest setting and DLSS on max performance. It ran OK for one session but it is so ugly it is almost pointless.  Loaded it up yesterday but the stuttering (even on low settings) just made me want to quit and do some work, it was that bad.  Went to load it this morning and 15 mins later it is still sitting there. I've given up and it is now unlikely I will ever play this game again. I'm done.  When it is working it is great, the sheer scale is amazing which is why I put so many hours in and have built some monster cities, packed with detail. As soon as I crank up the detail and settings though it just falls on its arse.   What is worth noting though that there are times when it doesn't even load or run properly with everything on the lowest settings. It isn't so much of a graphical problem, it is just the game is so poorly optimised that it fails right across from memory usage, CPU and GPU. The basic architecture is just not right.  With this game the developers have clearly made some major mistakes in its core design. In short, the tech cannot do what they want it to so it is compromised.  I see they are releasing new modules, so what? If the game doesn't run what is the point?  Don't bother.  I was a fan of the original game and Skylines 2 has far better road and transport design capabilities as well as far more detail, but it isn't an upgrade at all if it doesn't work.  A real shame.

It's not that great.  Playing the vanilla version makes it clear that the user experience is repetitive and boring. It feels like a slightly more realistic version of SimCity for mobile devices. In other words, it's nothing more than a tweaked, prettier version for moms who want a slightly fancier teatime setting. Ultimately, the concept of developing a city by balancing districts based on people's needs is a bit repetitive and not all that impressive.    The activities and movements of people around town are dull, with few interesting or exciting scenes.    What's particularly bad is the pathetically limited variety of buildings.  The business district lacks any quirky signboard names or hidden features from the developers; everything is scripted and boring.    I don't dislike CS2, but I didn't think there was much to it.  It's certainly not a game that stands out in any particular way.

This game is like buying a McDonalds burger after a night out drinking to discover the employee took three healthy bites out of it... leaving you with 80% of a burger and disappointment. Perhaps its just me but once you get a on a role, demand slows down despite doing what you need to.. the building options are meh, the feel just get you excited for what the city of your vision could be only to have it fall flat on delivery if you play for a lengthy period of time.  Maybe they will finish it one day without all these paid add ons... perhaps they didn't have the staff and need us to pay for them to finish it...
